---
title: "Plan: simulation-figure revision pass (Patrice's comments)"
date: 2026-06-15
format: html
---

Implementation plan for the simulation-figure changes from
`comments/ms_PP.docx` (see [[2026-06-15-patrice-ms-review-comments]]).
Decisions locked with Daniel 2026-06-15. Nothing implemented yet.

## A. Figure 2 — `fig-sims` (ms.qmd ~L284) — shape-violation bias/coverage

Currently 2 rows (scen3 "up varies with T", scen5 "k varies with T", `_n5`
cells), bias panel (a) + coverage panel (b), faceted `scenario_label × quantity`.

- **Add "Assumptions met" top row** (PP #117): bind `scen1` (strict-equivalence
  baseline, `_n5`) into `.d`; extend the `scenario_label` recode; set factor
  order top→bottom = **Assumptions met → up varies with T → k varies with T**.
  Shows all methods agreeing when assumptions hold (Normal-CI two-stage still
  under-covers ~0.86; t-CI and joint ~0.95–0.98), contrasting the rows below.
- **Coverage x-axis → 50–100%** (PP #117): `limits = c(0.5, 1)`,
  `breaks = c(0.5, 0.75, 0.95)`. Verified min coverage across scen1/3/5 n5 ≈ 0.59,
  so no points clip; re-confirm after adding scen1.
- **High-res export + untrimmed strip labels** (PP #117): mirror Fig 4/5 — `ggsave`
  a 600-dpi PNG and an `.svg` to `output/figs/`, then `include_graphics`. Raise
  `fig-height` ~6.5 → ~8 (third row + label room).
- Lowercase panel titles "a. Bias" / "b. Coverage" (PP #116).

## B. Figure 3 — `fig-sims2` (ms.qmd ~L408) — partial-mortality sweeps

Currently top = scen6 (β_up drift), bottom = scen7 (up0 constant-reduced),
bias-only, 4 distinct point estimators.

- **Reorder** to `.p7 / .p6` (simpler scenario on top) (PP #143).
- **Relabel rows** (PP #143): scen7 → "a. Upper asymptote differs from one";
  scen6 → "b. Upper asymptote drifts with temperature".
- **Simplify caption**: drop the explicit `{−0.005,…}` / `{0.65,…}` brace sets,
  give ranges instead (the "curly-bracket values" PP flagged) (PP #143).
- Confirm no bare "pp" remains; axis already reads "percentage points / °C"
  (PP #177). Keep the 4 point estimators (bias-only).
- High-res SVG export as in A.

## C. NEW Figure 4 — combined experimental-design figure (main text)

Daniel: one new main-text figure combining **both** design considerations.
Sources: `scen8` (sparse vs full grid × replication 1/3/5, 6 cells) and `scen9`
(max exposure cap 60/120/240/405 min, 4 caps). Data exist
(`per_sim_scen8_*`, `per_sim_scen9_*`) with bias + coverage.

**Layout (locked): bias + coverage, 2 rows.**

```
a. scen8 Bias (z|CTmax)      b. scen8 Coverage (z|CTmax)
c. scen9 Bias (z|CTmax)      d. scen9 Coverage (z|CTmax)
```

- **Methods (locked, "match the panel type"):** bias sub-panels = 4 distinct
  point estimators (two_stage_bin, two_stage_bb, joint relative, joint absolute);
  coverage sub-panels = all 6 (Normal vs t CIs differ in coverage). Shared legend
  spans all 6; bias panels simply omit the t-variants (identical bias). Note the
  mixed legend in the caption.
- Headline: joint 4PL stays ~unbiased and ~nominal across sparse designs, low
  replication, and short exposure caps; two-stage degrades badly (e.g. scen9 at
  60-min cap: two-stage coverage ~0.01–0.02 vs joint ~0.95–0.97; scen8 sparse×1:
  two-stage z RMSE ~32 vs joint ~2). This is PP's flagged "main selling point"
  (#106–108).
- High-res PNG + SVG export to `output/figs/`.
- **Supplement keeps its full versions** (all 6 methods, bias+coverage+RMSE,
  fig-sim-supp-scen8/9); main-text figure is the distilled headline. Cross-ref
  the supplement for full detail to avoid duplication confusion.
- **Renumbering:** this becomes Figure 4; case-study Figs 4→5, 5→6. Quarto
  renumbers `@fig-` refs automatically; update any plain-text figure mentions and
  add a results paragraph introducing it (tie to #106–108 take-home).

## D. Cross-cutting (all three figures)

- Lowercase "a./b." panel labels everywhere (PP #116).
- One sentence defining the Monte Carlo SE bars (PP #126): `mcse_bias = sd/√N`;
  `mcse_cov = √(p(1−p)/N)` — add to methods or each caption.
- High-res SVG export for all three sim figures (PP #117).
- `u` → `up` already done in these chunks' labels; keep new figure consistent.

## Verification
- Render `make ms-html` (or just exercise the chunks) and inspect: assumptions-met
  row reads correctly; coverage axis 0.5–1 clips nothing; new Fig 4 legible;
  cross-refs renumber cleanly. Confirm before declaring done (no "should work").
